operating_state: Net revenues declined 12% to $11.98 billion driven by Investment Banking falling 57% amid volatile conditions and rising rates reducing underwriting and M&A activity. Global Markets revenues increased 18% on higher FICC performance with significant client activity increases in interest rates, currencies, and commodities, while Equities declined on lower cash products performance. Consumer & Wealth Management grew while Other principal transactions fell 70% from reduced private equity gains. Total assets increased to $1.56 trillion driven by increases in investments, cash equivalents, and loans.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 17% driving efficiency ratio to 64.3% versus 48.4% prior year. Compensation expenses rose reflecting smaller year-over-year reduction in compensation-to-revenue ratio, while non-compensation expenses increased from NNIP and GreenSky acquisitions, higher litigation provisions, and transaction-based costs. Average daily VaR rose to $130 million from $80 million driven by higher volatility and expanded positions across interest rates, commodity prices, and currency rates.
material_events: Goldman returned $1.89 billion to shareholders through repurchases and dividends, with Board approving dividend increase from $2.00 to $2.50 per share. The firm announced organizational restructuring effective December 2022, consolidating from four segments to three. Goldman completed acquisitions of NNIP for European asset management, GreenSky for point-of-sale financing, and NextCapital Group for digital wealth management. The firm issued $2.5 billion in Fixed/Floating Rate Notes due 2028.
macro_exposures: Provision for credit losses increased to $515 million from $175 million reflecting consumer portfolio growth and macroeconomic concerns including inflation and Ukraine war impacts. Credit exposures to Russia declined to $205 million while Turkish exposure remained elevated. USD LIBOR transition exposure includes approximately $7 trillion notional derivative contracts with $5 trillion maturing after June 2023. Inflation Reduction Act introduced one percent excise tax on share repurchases after December 2022.
forward_outlook: Management targets medium-term efficiency ratio of approximately 60% while continuing growth investments. The firm maintains target of achieving annual firmwide management fees exceeding $10 billion by 2024 and $225 billion gross inflows for third-party alternatives business. Beginning Q4 2022, Goldman will report under new three-segment structure. Standardized CET1 capital ratio requirement increases to 13.8% effective January 2023 incorporating higher G-SIB surcharge from 2.5% to 3.0%.
uncertainty_profile: Economic outlook remains uncertain due to potential Russia-Ukraine conflict escalation, persistent inflation, supply chain disruptions, and central bank tightening affecting underwriting markets and client activity. Consumer credit losses may increase under adverse scenarios with potential allowance increases of approximately $1.0 billion under full adverse weighting. Regulatory liquidity requirements continue evolving with Basel framework implementation, while credit risk concentrations remain elevated with majority of loans rated BB or lower.
